meta.description: UX writing rules for MSM Portal. Defines tone, terminology, surface-specific rules, review criteria, and machine-readable checks for consistent product writing.

principles.name: Clear and specific
principles.rule: Say exactly what will happen. Prefer action-specific labels over generic verbs.

principles.name: Calm and professional
principles.rule: Use matter-of-fact language. Explain problems without blame or unnecessary urgency.

ko: 입력한 이메일 주소를 다시 확인해 주세요.
이 링크는 유효하지 않습니다. 설정을 다시 확인해 주세요.
en: Check the email address and try again.
This link is invalid. Check the settings and try again.

principles.name: Helpful guidance
principles.rule: When the user is blocked, explain the next step instead of only describing the problem.

ko: 타이틀을 입력하면 초안으로 저장할 수 있습니다.
워크플레이스를 선택한 뒤 다시 시도해 주세요.
en: Enter a title to save this as a draft.
Select a workplace and try again.

terminology.consistency_rule: Use the same noun for the same product concept across labels, empty states, dialogs, and toasts.

buttons_and_cta.rule: Label the result of the action, not the UI event.
guidance: Prefer 'Save changes' over 'Save' when the page edits an existing entity.
Use destructive labels that name the action, such as 'Delete order'.

labels_and_placeholders.rule: Labels describe the field. Placeholders give lightweight examples, not full instructions.
guidance: Keep field labels noun-based and stable.
Do not rely on placeholder text as the only explanation.
Avoid placeholder text that repeats the label word-for-word without adding value.

validation_and_errors.rule: Explain what is wrong and how to recover.
guidance: When possible, include the next action.
Avoid blameful language such as 'You entered an invalid value.'

good: 이메일 주소 형식을 다시 확인해 주세요.
제목을 입력하면 초안으로 저장할 수 있습니다.
Check the email address format and try again.
Enter a title to save this as a draft.

empty_states.rule: Empty states should explain what is missing and what the user can do next.
guidance: Provide a next step or CTA when available.
Avoid blank or purely decorative empty states.

dialogs_and_confirmations.rule: The title should name the decision. The body should explain the consequence.
guidance: Use a direct confirmation title for destructive actions.
Mention what will change, be removed, or stay the same.

toasts_and_status.rule: Toasts should be short and outcome-based. Status text should be stable and reusable.
guidance: Use success to confirm a completed action.
Use error to explain the failure and next step when possible.
Keep status nouns consistent with the domain terminology.

authoring_steps: Identify the user goal, not just the UI element.
Choose stable domain terminology before writing labels or errors.
Write the shortest copy that still explains the action or outcome.
Check the wording in Korean and English for parity of meaning, not literal word count.
Review the copy in context: label, button, dialog, empty state, or toast.
review_questions: Does the copy tell the user what will happen?
Would this still make sense without reading surrounding code?
If the action fails, does the user know the next step?
Is the same product concept named the same way across the screen?

automation_policy.rationale: UX writing quality needs human judgment, so automated checks should guide reviewers without blocking legitimate domain-specific wording.

automated_checks.description: Warn when a locale string contains discouraged tone markers such as blameful or unnecessarily forceful wording.
Warn when submit-style action keys use generic verbs instead of the concrete outcome.

manual_review: Confirm that button labels reflect the real action on the current screen.
Confirm that empty states and error states include recovery guidance.
Review key copy in both Korean and English for consistent meaning.
Check destructive dialogs for consequence clarity and button specificity.

discouraged_phrases.message: Avoid 'simply' in product copy. It can sound dismissive.
Avoid 'obviously' in product copy. It can sound blameful.
discouraged_phrases.suggestion: Explain the action without judging the user.

discouraged_phrases.message: Avoid urgent phrasing unless the task is truly time-sensitive.

generic_cta_rules.message: Replace generic 'Submit' with the concrete action outcome.
generic_cta_rules.suggestion: Use wording like 'Send test link', 'Save changes', or 'Submit order'.

generic_cta_rules.message: Replace generic '보내기' with the concrete action outcome.
generic_cta_rules.suggestion: '테스트 링크 보내기', '변경 사항 저장', '주문 제출'처럼 결과가 드러나는 문구를 사용하세요.

button_labels.why: The improved label tells the user exactly what will happen.
The improved label matches the edit intent and sets a clearer expectation.

error_messages.scenario: Required title for saving a draft

after.ko: 타이틀을 입력하면 초안으로 저장할 수 있습니다.
after.en: Enter a title to save this as a draft.

error_messages.why: The improved message explains the recovery step.

after.ko: 예약형 주문이 없습니다. 새 주문을 만들어 시작해 보세요.
after.en: There are no reserved orders yet. Create a new order to get started.

empty_states.why: The improved empty state names the missing content and suggests the next action.
